name: Payne Law Firm
description_short: Memphis-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7, 13, and 11 filings with 25+ years of experience. Offers free consultations and personalized attorney-led representation.
description_long:
Payne Law Firm is a bankruptcy legal practice founded and operated by Jerome C. Payne, who earned his Juris Doctorate in 1993 and has been admitted to practice law since that year. The firm is located in Memphis, Tennessee at 3525 Ridge Meadow Parkway and has served the local community for more than 25 years, assisting thousands of families with bankruptcy filings and debt relief.

The firm specializes in three primary bankruptcy chapters: Chapter 7 (liquidation/straight bankruptcy), Chapter 13 (wage earner bankruptcy with debt repayment plans), and Chapter 11 (business reorganization including Subchapter V for small businesses). Beyond bankruptcy filing, they offer strategic alternatives including motions to stop garnishments and levies without filing, foreclosure defense, eviction halting via Chapter 13, and car repossession prevention or recovery. Each case receives personal attention from the attorneys, with clients guided through every step of the process.

Payne Law Firm distinguishes itself through direct attorney involvement rather than paralegal-heavy case management, a commitment to assessing whether bankruptcy actually benefits each client before recommending it, and multimedia client education including a regular Facebook Live show called "Talking Law" featuring Attorney Payne. The firm transparently addresses common client concerns through detailed FAQ content covering debt discharge eligibility, asset protection, creditor notification, and realistic timelines (3-6 months typical completion).

The firm is legitimate and established, with verifiable contact information, a professional website, and transparent pricing structure (free consultations). However, consumers should note this is a legal service requiring payment for representation, and bankruptcy has serious long-term credit consequences. The website content is informational rather than aggressive in sales approach, suggesting ethical business practices.
address: 3525 Ridge Meadow Pkwy Ste 100, Memphis, TN 38115

company_info.headquarters: 3525 Ridge Meadow Pkwy Ste 100, Memphis, TN 38115

pros: 25+ years of established service in Memphis community with thousands of assisted families
Free initial consultation with no obligation to file bankruptcy
Personal attorney involvement—Jerome C. Payne handles cases directly rather than delegating to paralegals
Comprehensive service range covering Chapter 7, 13, and 11 bankruptcies plus business reorganization
Explores alternatives to bankruptcy (garnishment/levy motions) before recommending filing
Clear communication of realistic timelines (3-6 months) and honest assessment of debt discharge eligibility
Educational outreach through weekly Facebook Live and cable TV show to build client knowledge
cons: No pricing transparency on website—legal fees not disclosed, requiring direct inquiry
Limited geographic service area (Memphis-focused), unclear if they serve outside Tennessee
Website content incomplete—car repossession section cuts off mid-sentence, suggesting poor website maintenance
No information about bankruptcy trustee relationships, success rates, or case outcomes
Heavy reliance on personal brand (Jerome C. Payne) creates dependency on single attorney availability
best_for: Memphis-area residents facing Chapter 7 liquidation with unsecured debts exceeding assets
Wage earners seeking Chapter 13 repayment plans to retain property (home, vehicle)
Small business owners needing Chapter 11 or Subchapter V reorganization without liquidation
Consumers with garnishments, levies, foreclosure threats, or eviction notices needing immediate legal intervention
diagnosis: Payne Law Firm is best for Memphis-area residents and small business owners needing legitimate bankruptcy representation with personal attorney involvement and honest pre-filing assessment. The critical caveat is that bankruptcy is a legal proceeding with severe 7-10 year credit report consequences; this firm should only be engaged after exploring all alternatives, and consumers must understand they are paying for legal services, not quick debt elimination.
services: Chapter 7 Bankruptcy filing and representation (straight/liquidation bankruptcy)
Chapter 13 Bankruptcy filing (wage earner plans with debt repayment)
Chapter 11 Bankruptcy for business debt reorganization
Subchapter V Chapter 11 for small business relief
Garnishment and levy prevention/relief via Motion to Set Payments
Foreclosure defense and temporary relief strategies
Eviction halting via Chapter 13 with landlord payment plan negotiation
Bankruptcy alternatives assessment and strategic planning
Case management and creditor notification coordination
Creditor harassment cessation via automatic stay
